title: Vic (43, Pension Portfolio Manager)
learning_objective: Practice handling defensiveness and building rapport with a high-performing but resistant colleague.
situation: Vic is a proud professional with 5 years at Money and Pensions Service. He consistently meets targets and believes he's one of the strongest performers, though colleagues have mentioned collaboration skills. He values being seen to give excellent service but is skeptical of 'soft' feedback.

title: Mary (34, Customer Service Rep)
learning_objective: Practice using empathy and affirmation to support a struggling team member.
situation: Mary was Customer Service Rep of the Year in 2022, but performance has slipped recently. As a single mother, she's naturally caring but currently overwhelmed by competing work and family demands.

title: Jan (28, Customer Service Rep)
learning_objective: Practice engaging a withdrawn colleague to understand root causes of performance changes.
situation: Jan was previously a strong performer (even employee of the month) but recent metrics have dropped. They're quiet, self-critical, and genuinely confused about the decline. Personal changes may be affecting work, though they haven't made that connection yet.

title: Terry (42, Pension Regulations Expert)
learning_objective: Practice focusing the conversation on behavioral change with a direct, expertise-focused colleague.
situation: Terry has 15 years experience as the pension regulations go-to person. Highly competent and efficiency-focused, but colleagues find their communication style too direct and sometimes intimidating. They're frustrated by 'soft skills' feedback.
